title: Vault mirror rotted silently for ~6 months (per-ticket briefs frozen at T-115) - sync daily via scheduler, not only at session exit
source: Claude Fable improvement pass 2026-07-07 - vault/notes/per-ticket/ stops at T-115 while tickets/closed/ is at T-283; PI.md section 2 tells every session to read those briefs
current_state: sync_vault() (tools/tools_obsidian.py:32 -> render_per_ticket_notes at :792) regenerates the vault mirror including one brief per closed ticket - but it only runs at session exit, and the daemon has not executed a clean exit-sync in months. Result: ~170 closed tickets have no vault brief, the Foam graph renders a May-2026 snapshot, and PI.md section 2's documented read path ('vault/notes/per-ticket/T-NNN-slug.md') silently serves stale or missing files. Nothing detects the rot: doc_drift_watcher checks PI.md counts, not vault freshness.
target_state: (a) A daily scheduler job in tools/tools_scheduler.py (clone the _turns_log_rotate_job pattern, e.g. 03:45) calls sync_vault() so the mirror refreshes even when session exits don't happen. (b) doc_drift_watcher gains one check: the highest ticket id in tickets/closed/ has a matching file in vault/notes/per-ticket/ - WARN naming the gap size if not. Test: unit test the new drift check with a tmp tree (closed ticket present, brief absent -> WARN; both present -> PASS); scheduler job smoke-tested like the existing rotate-job test.
migration_plan: 1. Test first: extend testing/test_doc_drift_watcher.py with the brief-freshness check cases (tmp closed-tickets dir + tmp vault dir).
2. Add the freshness check to scripts/passive/doc_drift_watcher.py (compare max closed T-NNN vs per-ticket filenames - pure filesystem, no network).
3. Add _vault_sync_job to tools/tools_scheduler.py: schedule.every().day.at('03:45'), calling tools_obsidian.sync_vault with try/except + track_silent('scheduler.vault_sync', e).
4. Run sync_vault() once by hand to backfill the ~170 missing briefs; confirm vault/notes/per-ticket/ reaches T-283.
5. python scripts/verify.py must print PASS (never pipe, T-214); SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close; refresh_pi.py.
risk_notes: sync_vault touches many files - the scheduler job must be try/except + track_silent so a sync failure never kills the scheduler thread. Do NOT make the passive check FAIL-level: a stale mirror is a WARN (Pi's real memory is unaffected - the vault is a read cache by design). One-way sync stays one-way; do not add write-back.
